title: CVT Structure Description - Shift Operation Mechanism

blocks.text: CVT Structure Description - Shift Operation Mechanism

The select lever is a straight five-position instrument panel shift mounted on the instrument panel (P, R, N, D, L: models without paddle shift switches or P, R, N, D, S: models with paddle shift switches). A shift lock mechanism is installed on the select lever to prevent incorrect operation of the select lever when starting. As part of the key interlock system, a parking pin switch is mounted on the select lever to signal the driver MICU (Multiplex Integrated Control Unit) that the select lever is in the P position. The signal is transmitted to the driver MICU (Multiplex Integrated Control Unit) as a signal that the select lever is in P position.

The shift lock mechanism is a device that prevents the select lever from being moved from the P position to other positions unless the brake pedal is depressed and the accelerator pedal is not depressed after starting the engine in the P position.
The shift lock solenoid is normally OFF and the shift lock stopper connected to the shift lock solenoid plunger is in the P position.

When moving the select lever from the P position, pressing the push button on the select lever lowers the lock pin, disengages the lock pin from the P position gate, and allows the select lever to move. After starting the engine in the P position, if the brake pedal is not depressed, the shift lock solenoid is OFF and the shift lock stopper locks the lock pin in the P position, so the select lever cannot be moved from the P position. When the brake pedal is depressed and the accelerator pedal is not depressed, the shift lock solenoid is ON and the shift lock solenoid plunger is retracted to move the shift lock stopper from the P position position to release the lock pin, allowing the select lever to move.

If the shift lock cannot be released due to a shift lock solenoid failure or electrical malfunction, insert a key or other object through the shift lock release hole in the shift position indicator panel and press the shift lock release to release the shift lock. When the shift lock release is pressed, the shift lock release pushes the shift lock stopper to release the lock pin.
